parameters.query:
INSERT INTO job_search_vacancies (title, company, link, ai_score, ai_reason)
VALUES (
  $${{ $json.title }}$$, 
  $${{ $json.company }}$$, 
  $${{ $json.link }}$$,
  {{ $json.ai_score }},
  $${{ $json.ai_reason }}$$
);

parameters.text:
=Act as a professional data analyst. Filter and transform the provided job list into a strictly valid JSON array.

### FILTERING CRITERIA:
1. ONLY include jobs related to: No-code, Low-code, Automation, AI, Software Development (Python, JS, etc.), or Data Engineering.
2. EXCLUDE everything else: Sales, Marketing, HR, Logistics, Construction, or Manual Management.
3. The input may include jobs from multiple sources, including Djinni, DOU, and Work.ua (possibly from multiple queries or pages). Include all relevant jobs.
4. If no jobs match these categories, return an empty array [].

### SCORING & NOTES:
- Assign "ai_score" from 1.0 to 5.0 based on relevance to Automation/AI/No-code.
- For each job, add a short sentence in "ai_reason" (in Ukrainian) explaining why you gave this score.
- Make sure the description is clean, max 200 characters.

### RULES:
1. Output ONLY the raw JSON array. No explanations, no markdown blocks.
2. Fields to create for each job:
   - "title": Job title.
   - "company": Company name.
   - "link": Full URL.
   - "description": Short summary (max 200 chars).
   - "source": "Djinni", "DOU", or "Work.ua".
   - "ai_score": Rate from 1.0 to 5.0.
   - "ai_reason": Short sentence in Ukrainian explaining the relevance.

### DATA TO PROCESS:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.all_jobs) }}

parameters.name: User-Agent
parameters.value: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36
